title: Recursive latent degradation state plus uncertainty features
summary: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows value from probabilistic state-space updates, not just observed tyre age.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows value from probabilistic state-space updates, not just observed tyre age. That suggests the useful signal may be the filtered hidden pace loss and its uncertainty, rather than more fixed compound-life cutoffs.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race stint, build lightweight online features from a recursive filter on lap-time residuals after subtracting race-lap and compound baselines: filtered degradation level, slope, shock term, and posterior uncertainty proxy. Reset at `PitStop==1`; never use future laps. Feed these features into the existing tabular learner instead of replacing it with a full Bayesian model.
expected_effect: Should separate true wear pressure from noisy single-lap spikes, especially where `LapTime_Delta` is volatile; uncertainty may help flag imminent-stop regimes.
risk: Very similar in spirit to earlier latent-degradation proposals, so it needs to be operationalized more simply and tested for incremental value over existing degradation ratios. Poor baseline subtraction could just repackage noise.
